body:
MORNING INTEL — 2026-07-13 (snapshot 2026-07-13_12)
Regime data: VIX 17.11 | HY OAS 269 | Breadth 63.6%
AMZN: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
NVDA: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
SPY: TRADE | NEUTRAL conf 51.666666666666664% | Range 729.12–769.04 | iron_condor x1
Portfolio risk after fills: pending Artha confirmation
Discipline: 30d system PnL $0 | override PnL $0
Data quality: all sources OK

structured.takeaway: The current regime shows elevated volatility with VIX at 17.11, a steep 3-month contango, and HY OAS at 269 bps, signaling persistent risk aversion amid rising yields (US10Y 4.54%) and a firm dollar (DXY 101.28). SPY is under pressure at $749.08 with negative sentiment (-2), dragged down by escalating U.S.-Iran tensions that have pushed the Nasdaq sharply lower, while NVDA at $203.53 and AMZN at $247.31 stand out with positive sentiment (+2 and +1 respectively) amid a thematic generational transfer favoring semiconductor leaders and deep-value tech. The near-term catalyst to watch is the geopolitical escalation and its impact on tech and growth sectors, as well as the upcoming heavy slate of mega-bank earnings starting tomorrow, which could either confirm or alleviate fears of a hawkish Fed pivot given recent inflation signals. The key risk is that persistent geopolitical volatility combined with hawkish Fed rhetoric may trigger a broader risk-off move, further pressuring high-beta growth names and keeping volatility elevated. Trade signal gates remain fully active, favoring selective long exposure in structurally advantaged tech names like NVDA while maintaining caution on broad market longs.

SPY.sentiment_driver: Nasdaq closes sharply lower as stocks fall

NVDA.sentiment_driver: NVIDIA and Micron the Winners in generational transfer
NVDA.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

AMZN.sentiment_driver: Amazon stock hasn't been this cheap in over a decade
AMZN.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

news.title: Nasdaq closes sharply lower as stocks fall after latest U.S.-Iran escalation
Nasdaq leading stocks lower in final hour as U.S.-Iran tensions escalate — live
Inflation to fall for first time in 6 years. Consumers won't see much relief.
A Generational Transfer Is Happening: Amazon, Google, Microsoft Are the Losers. NVIDIA and Micron the Winners.
Amazon Stock Hasn't Been This Cheap in Over a Decade. Has the Sell-Off Gone Too Far?
A hot inflation reading this week could mean a rate hike soon, Fed’s Waller says
Nasdaq and S&P 500 open lower as Iran launches new strikes
Why Citigroup is the one to watch when banks report earnings this week
Economic calendar: Treasury balance today; CPI and PPI this week
India's inflation accelerates to 4.38% in June, exceeding forecasts
The stock-market rally now hinges more on AI than oil
Oil prices rise, stock futures dip after latest flare-up of U.S.-Iran strikes

narratives.this_week: Bank earnings dominate this week with JPM, BAC, WFC, GS, and MS reporting between July 14-15. Investors should focus on credit quality and trading revenue for signals on economic resilience and financial conditions. Health care names like JNJ and UNH also report, with UNH’s bullish outlook potentially supporting defensive sectors in SPY amid ongoing macro uncertainty.
narratives.next_week: Next week features key tech and communication services earnings, notably GOOGL, TSLA, and T, alongside industrials and materials like RTX and FCX. GOOGL and TSLA’s performance will be critical for gauging demand trends and AI adoption, influencing NVDA’s momentum given its AI accelerator dominance. Real estate earnings from CCI and DLR may provide insights into interest rate sensitivity, impacting broader equity sentiment.
narratives.next_month: Looking further ahead, the focus shifts to mega-cap tech with MSFT, META, AMZN, and AAPL reporting late July, alongside the July 30 FOMC meeting and July 31 PCE inflation data. The FOMC’s likely hold and elevated core PCE readings could pressure growth stocks including AMZN and NVDA, while MSFT’s bullish stance may offer some sector support. Investors should also monitor AMD’s AI revenue guidance for competitive dynamics within tech.
